title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 30, 2026
headline: DRC Ebola spread to a fourth province June 30 — 1,307 cases, 377 deaths, the fastest-growing outbreak on record; Israel struck Lebanon 83 dead on ceasefire-renewal day.
summary: The Democratic Republic of Congo's Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak spread to Haut-Uele province on June 30 — a fourth province affected — raising confirmed cases to 1,307 and deaths to 377. Haut-Uele borders South Sudan and the Central African Republic. South Sudan sits at the absolute floor at 0 of 100 for separate crises. This is the first scenario in the June series where two floor-level countries share an active epidemiological frontier. No approved vaccine for this Ebola strain exists. The DRC's score of 2.3 of 100 is confirmed. On the same day, Israeli forces launched more than 100 airstrikes on Lebanon since midnight, killing 83 and wounding 141 — on the same day the ceasefire framework was renewed. Hezbollah rejected that framework. The 83 deaths are Israel's conduct, scored against Israel. Israel holds at 0 of 100. Lebanon holds at 17.2 of 100. Somalia entered the priority assessment cycle for the first time in the June series. Its score of 4.7 of 100 is confirmed. Buur Hakaba district has reached IPC Phase 5 — the extreme acute malnutrition threshold — with a Global Acute Malnutrition rate of 37.1 percent. For the first time in the series, all eight Critical-band entities at or near the absolute floor were assessed in a single cycle: Sudan, Israel, Yemen, and South Sudan at 0 of 100; DRC at 2.3, Iran at 2.5, Somalia at 4.7, and Burkina Faso at 6.3. Zero score changes were proposed. This is the ninth consecutive cycle with an empty proposal queue.

topSignals.title: DRC 2.3 of 100: Ebola Spread to a Fourth Province June 30 — 1,307 Cases, 377 Deaths; Haut-Uele Now Borders South Sudan at the Absolute Floor
topSignals.whyItMatters: DRC's Ebola outbreak reached a fourth province June 30, making it the fastest-growing Bundibugyo outbreak ever recorded. Haut-Uele, the new province, borders South Sudan — which is already at the lowest possible score for separate crises.
topSignals.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo holds at 2.3 of 100. The Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak expanded to Haut-Uele province on June 30, the fourth province affected in DRC's northeast. Confirmed cases rose to 1,307 with 377 deaths — up from 1,155 cases and 304 deaths on June 25. Uganda has recorded 19 cases. Approximately 15 million people across four provinces are now in the outbreak zone. The June 30 assessment describes this as the fastest-growing Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak ever recorded. No approved vaccine for the Bundibugyo strain exists. Contact tracing stands at approximately 45 percent — about half the rate needed to stop transmission. Community mistrust is impeding safe burials. The government's response capacity — the scored element — remains weak. M23's eastern mass-killing harm is attributed to Rwanda, not DRC, and is not scored against DRC. DRC's near-floor score of 2.3 prices its existing response weakness. The floor-crossing trigger requires documented deliberate government obstruction of WHO response corridors. That has not occurred. DRC is confirmed at 2.3 of 100; confidence: high. Haut-Uele borders both South Sudan and the Central African Republic — two countries in severe crisis. South Sudan sits at 0 of 100 for acute food insecurity affecting 7.8 million people (56 percent of the population), 2.2 million children with acute malnutrition, and four counties at famine risk. A realized Ebola incursion into South Sudan, combined with documented failure of South Sudan's own response, would constitute scorable own-conduct. This is the first scenario in the June 2026 series where two entities at or near the absolute floor share an active epidemiological frontier.

evidence.claim: WHO Ebola DRC situation report: 1,307 confirmed cases and 377 deaths as of June 30; outbreak spread to Haut-Uele province, the fourth province affected in DRC's northeast; Bundibugyo strain; no approved vaccine; WHO PHEIC active since May 16; contact tracing at approximately 45 percent.

ECDC June 2026: first EU-imported case (France, humanitarian worker, June 24); second imported case (Germany); fastest-growing Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak on record; Haut-Uele borders South Sudan and the Central African Republic; 19 cases in Uganda.

topSignals.title: Lebanon 17.2 and Israel 0 of 100: 83 Killed, 141 Wounded from 100-Plus Israeli Airstrikes on Ceasefire-Renewal Day — Attribution to Israel Confirmed
topSignals.whyItMatters: Israeli forces launched more than 100 airstrikes on Lebanon June 30, killing 83 and wounding 141 — on the same day the ceasefire framework was renewed. The 83 deaths are Israel's conduct, scored against Israel. Lebanon is evaluated on its own conduct only.
topSignals.description: Lebanon holds at 17.2 of 100. Israel holds at 0 of 100. Israeli forces launched more than 100 airstrikes on Lebanon since midnight June 30. Eighty-three people were killed and 141 wounded. A drone struck Nabatieh. These strikes occurred on the same day the Lebanon ceasefire framework was renewed. Hezbollah rejected the framework. The benchmark scores each government on its own conduct. Israel launched the strikes; the 83 deaths are Israel's own negative conduct, scored against Israel. Lebanon is evaluated on what Lebanon's government does — or fails to do — for its own people and civilian protection. No new Lebanese government harm occurred June 30 that would drive a downgrade. No durable ceasefire or Lebanese Armed Forces sovereign control was established that would drive an upgrade. Lebanon is confirmed at 17.2 of 100; confidence: medium. Israel's floor at 0 of 100 is reinforced by the June 30 strikes. The UN Commission of Inquiry (report A/HRC/62/CRP.2, June 23) found that Israel continues to commit genocide by deliberately targeting Palestinian children — approximately 20,000 killed and 44,000 injured since October 2023. A total of 3,338-plus ceasefire violations have been logged through June 20. The Kerem Shalom crossing is congested and the Zikim crossing has been closed for two weeks. Israel is confirmed at 0 of 100; confidence: high. The forward trigger for Lebanon remains the same: a new ceasefire agreement combined with Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control of southern Lebanon and delivering reconstruction access.

evidence.claim: Al Jazeera June 30: Israeli forces launched more than 100 airstrikes on Lebanon since midnight; 83 killed, 141 wounded; drone struck Nabatieh; Hezbollah rejected the ceasefire framework renewed on the same day; harm attributed to Israel's own conduct.
Al Jazeera June 23 on UN COI report A/HRC/62/CRP.2: approximately 20,000 Palestinian children killed, 44,000 injured since October 2023; genocide determination issued and remains on the record.

topSignals.title: Somalia 4.7 of 100: First Priority Assessment in the June Series — Near-Floor Confirmed; Buur Hakaba at IPC AMN Phase 5, GAM Rate 37.1 Percent
topSignals.whyItMatters: Somalia entered the priority assessment cycle for the first time June 30 and its score of 4.7 of 100 is confirmed. Buur Hakaba district has reached the extreme acute malnutrition threshold. One in three people in the analyzed population faces Crisis or worse. The end-of-June famine declaration window closed without a formal issuance.
topSignals.description: Somalia holds at 4.7 of 100. This is the first time Somalia has entered the priority assessment cycle in the June 2026 series, replacing Saudi Arabia from June 29. Buur Hakaba (Burhakaba) district has reached IPC AMN Phase 5 — the Extremely Critical acute malnutrition threshold — with a Global Acute Malnutrition rate of 37.1 percent. Baidoa district also sits at IPC AMN Phase 5. Approximately 1.88 million children require acute malnutrition treatment in 2026. Roughly six million people — about one in three of the analyzed population — face Crisis or worse. The World Food Programme is reaching approximately one in ten people who need food. Drought from poor Gu rains, regional price shocks linked to the 2026 Middle East conflict, currency depreciation, and Al-Shabaab attacks are largely exogenous drivers. Only the federal government's own response capacity is scorable. That capacity remains within the existing near-floor band. No formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration was issued on or before June 30. The end-of-June threshold window passed without issuance. The famine-declaration risk window extends through September 2026. Somalia's inclusion tonight completes the full eight-entity floor and near-floor picture in a single assessment cycle — the first time this has happened in the series. Somalia is confirmed at 4.7 of 100; confidence: medium.

evidence.claim: Action Against Hunger June 2026: Buur Hakaba district at IPC AMN Phase 5 (Extremely Critical) with Global Acute Malnutrition rate of 37.1 percent — past the famine threshold; approximately 1.88 million children require acute malnutrition treatment in Somalia in 2026.

IPC June 2026: Baidoa and Buur Hakaba both at IPC AMN Phase 5; approximately six million people face IPC Phase 3 Crisis or worse; WFP reaching approximately one in ten people in need in Somalia; end-of-June formal famine declaration window passed without issuance.

topSignals.title: Venezuela 18.0 of 100: Day 6 Earthquake Response — Toll Rises to 1,719 Dead, 46,600 Missing; US Doubles Commitment to 300 Million Dollars
topSignals.whyItMatters: Venezuela's earthquake death toll rose to 1,719 and missing persons to 46,600 by Day 6. The United States doubled its relief commitment to 300 million dollars. A UNICEF 48-tonne supply shipment arrived June 30. The score holds at 18.0 of 100. Both upgrade and downgrade triggers remain unmet.
topSignals.description: Venezuela holds at 18.0 of 100. Day 6 of the response to the June 24 twin earthquakes (magnitude 7.2 and 7.5). The death toll rose to 1,719 — up from approximately 1,450 on June 29. Reported missing persons reached 46,600. More than 3,150 were injured. The New York Times reported a likely substantial undercount, which is an early allegation, not documented suppression. UNICEF's second supply shipment — 48 tonnes — arrived June 30; the first 20-tonne shipment had landed June 27. The United States doubled its humanitarian commitment to 300 million dollars (from 150 million dollars under OFAC General License 60 issued June 25). These are donor commitments, not Venezuelan government compassion conduct. The scored question is what Venezuela's own government does to protect and assist its people. Aid acceptance from international sources continues — that separates Venezuela from floor-cluster states. Domestic relief remains slow, disjointed, and opaque. Access restrictions from approximately 14,000 troops and police remain. Both screening rules continue to apply. The directionality rule blocks an upgrade: aid acceptance alone does not clear the upgrade bar when the entity surfaced on a negative event. The double-count rule blocks a downgrade: the published score of 18.0 already prices Venezuela's weak institutional capacity and accountability record. The July 8 checkpoint — the 14-day mark from the June 24 earthquake — is the next decisive assessment date. The scored downgrade trigger remains documented deliberate aid obstruction or diversion. Venezuela is confirmed at 18.0 of 100; confidence: medium.

evidence.claim: UNICEF USA June 30, 2026: UNICEF's second supply shipment — 48 tonnes — arrived in Venezuela June 30; first 20-tonne shipment arrived June 27; UNICEF continues emergency support call for children affected by the earthquakes.

US State Department June 25, 2026: OFAC General License 60 authorizes Venezuela earthquake relief transactions through October 23, 2026; 150-million-dollar commitment through UN and faith-based channels — commitment subsequently doubled to 300 million dollars.

CNN June 30: toll rises to 1,719 dead, 46,600 missing, 3,150-plus injured by Day 6; New York Times reports likely substantial undercount — early allegation, not documented suppression; government domestic relief remains slow and access-restricted.

topSignals.title: Floor Cluster Fully Documented June 30 — All Eight Critical-Band Entities at or Near 0 Assessed in One Cycle for the First Time
topSignals.whyItMatters: Sudan, Israel, Yemen, and South Sudan all sit at 0 of 100. DRC sits at 2.3, Iran at 2.5, Somalia at 4.7, and Burkina Faso at 6.3. All eight were assessed in the same cycle June 30 — the first time in the June series. No score moved. All threshold events that would produce a change remain unresolved.
topSignals.description: Sudan holds at 0 of 100. El Obeid is in its 18th-plus day under RSF drone siege, with more than 500,000 civilians trapped. Al-Burhan's June 29 rejection of the Quad humanitarian ceasefire closes the near-term SAF-side diplomatic path. The US State Department raised an imminent risk of mass atrocities warning. No new development beyond June 29 occurred to move Sudan's score. Israel holds at 0 of 100, reinforced by the June 30 Lebanon strikes. Yemen holds at 0 of 100. Houthi authorities forced the termination of all 365 World Food Programme northern-area staff contracts. Seventy-three UN workers remain detained; one has died in custody. Aid assets are seized. Twenty-two point three million people need humanitarian assistance. Only 12.7 percent of the 2.16-billion-dollar appeal is funded. Doha talks are a forward factor, not a current own-conduct score move for Yemen. South Sudan holds at 0 of 100, with the DRC Ebola spread to Haut-Uele now the highest-priority new cross-border forward risk. Iran holds at 2.5 of 100. The Strait of Hormuz central channel is mined and Iran is demanding transit fees, with hundreds of vessels stranded. The execution surge stands at 784-plus year-to-date — a 37-year high. Burkina Faso holds at 6.3 of 100, with the Sahel lean-season crisis and the Sahel calibration flag (day seven) continuing. The nine-consecutive-cycle zero-proposal record extends. The threshold events that would generate proposals — Somalia formal famine declaration, DRC Ebola floor-crossing, Sudan ceasefire implementation, Venezuela aid obstruction documentation — remain unresolved.

evidence.claim: UN News June 27: Under-Secretary-General DiCarlo warned the window to prevent mass atrocities in El Obeid is 'rapidly narrowing'; 500,000-plus civilians besieged; UNSC action demanded; no enforcement resolution issued.

UN News June 2026: WFP terminated all 365 local-staff contracts in northern Yemen (covering 70 percent of humanitarian needs); 73 UN workers detained, one died in custody; aid assets seized; 12.7 percent of the 2.16-billion-dollar appeal funded.

boundaryWatch.trigger: EU AI Act full applicability on August 2 — 33 days away. Next US Commerce or BIS action on the Fable 5 export-control order. Mythos 5 partially unblocked June 26 for approximately 100 US companies and agencies.
boundaryWatch.note: Anthropic sits 0.9 points below the Functional-to-Established boundary at 60.0. Tenth consecutive cycle in this range. Day 19 of the export-control suspension. Fable 5 remains fully suspended globally. EU AI Act applies August 2 — 33 days from tonight.

boundaryWatch.trigger: NLRB review of the unfair-labor-practice charge filed after Apple closed its Towson, Maryland store — its first unionized store. An NLRB complaint or ruling is the conversion trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Apple sits 0.6 points below the Functional-to-Established boundary at 60.0. The Congressional Labor Caucus call for an NLRB investigation remains pre-adjudication. Sixth boundary-watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented discriminatory denial of emergency protection to bidoon or migrant workers during wartime — Equity downgrade trigger. Durable verified expansion of bidoon rights or citizenship recognition — upgrade trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Kuwait at 25.0 Developing sits 5.0 points above the Critical-to-Developing boundary. EQU watch flag open on bidoon and migrant-worker emergency-protection performance under active Iran war conditions. Day 2 of boundary watch.

boundaryWatch.trigger: July natural Ebola peak window — active. Haut-Uele spread documented June 30 — now bordering South Sudan. Documented government obstruction of WHO response corridors is the floor-crossing trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: DRC at 2.3 Critical through cycle 17 of the Ebola emergency. 1,307 cases and 377 deaths as of June 30 — up from 1,155 and 304 on June 25. Outbreak spread to a fourth province, Haut-Uele, which borders South Sudan and the Central African Republic. No approved Bundibugyo vaccine. Contact tracing at approximately 45 percent.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration covering Buur Hakaba or Baidoa. Both districts at IPC AMN Phase 5. End-of-June declaration window passed without issuance. Famine risk window extends through September 2026.
boundaryWatch.note: Somalia at 4.7 Critical. Buur Hakaba at IPC AMN Phase 5 with GAM rate 37.1 percent. First priority assessment completed this cycle. End-of-June threshold date passed without a formal famine declaration. Famine risk window extends through September 2026. Seventh cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Coordinator-level Sahel-band calibration review. Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100) was reassessed in June on comparable UN-documented state-perpetration conduct. Mali's starting-point score of 12.5 is above the likely calibrated range.
boundaryWatch.note: Mali at 12.5 Critical on a starting-point seed score. The Sahel calibration flag is in its seventh consecutive day. The gap with Burkina Faso (6.3, reweighted June 1 on active-state-perpetration conduct) persists. Coordinator review covers Mali, Niger, and potentially Chad. No timeline set.

boundaryWatch.trigger: DOJ False Claims Act complaint (kickbacks and disability discrimination) and Barrows v. Humana class action (nH Predict AI claim denials). Either concluded finding confirms the Developing-band designation.
boundaryWatch.note: Humana at 35.2 Developing, 4.8 points below the Functional boundary. Ninth cycle post-downgrade. Not assessed this cycle. Neither the DOJ complaint nor the Barrows class action has a fixed hearing date.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Bengali Muslim and Rohingya expulsions documented in June 2026; detect-delete-deport digital surveillance continuing. Not assessed this cycle.
boundaryWatch.note: India at 15.6 Critical sits 0.6 points above the Critical floor through a sixteenth consecutive monitoring cycle. Not assessed tonight.

emergingRisks.risk: DRC Ebola spread to Haut-Uele province June 30, opening the first epidemiological frontier between two floor-level countries in the June series. Haut-Uele borders South Sudan (0 of 100) and the Central African Republic. Fifteen million people in DRC's northeast are now in the outbreak zone.
emergingRisks.detail: The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak reached 1,307 cases and 377 deaths as of June 30. Contact tracing runs at approximately 45 percent. No approved vaccine exists. If the outbreak crosses into South Sudan — which is already at the absolute floor for acute food insecurity, famine-risk counties, and child malnutrition — and South Sudan's own response proves inadequate, that government response failure would constitute scorable own-conduct. This is the highest-priority new forward risk in the June series.

emergingRisks.risk: The Lebanon-Israel conflict produced 83 deaths and 141 wounded from 100-plus Israeli airstrikes on June 30 — on ceasefire-renewal day. Hezbollah rejected the renewed framework. The trajectory points toward continued strikes without a new diplomatic structure.
emergingRisks.detail: Israel's Boundaries (BND) and Action (ACT) dimensions remain the active watched dimensions for Lebanon: specifically, Lebanese Armed Forces sovereign control versus Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, and civilian protection under continued strikes. The forward trigger for Lebanon is unchanged: a new ceasefire agreement combined with Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control and delivering reconstruction access. A further escalation in Lebanon civilian casualties from Israeli strikes reinforces Israel's floor at 0 of 100 without moving Lebanon.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan's El Obeid siege entered its 18th-plus day June 30. The US State Department issued an imminent risk of mass atrocities warning. Al-Burhan's June 29 ceasefire rejection closes the near-term SAF-side diplomatic path. The RSF drone-then-assault pattern killed roughly 6,000 civilians in El-Fasher in three days in May 2025.
emergingRisks.detail: More than 500,000 civilians remain besieged in El Obeid. Hospitals are offline. Famine is declared in El-Fasher and Kadugli. Sudan holds at 0 of 100. Al-Burhan's rejection means a SAF-implemented ceasefire is unlikely in the near term. The RSF's prior acceptance of the Quad proposal stands as the current forward trigger — observed RSF unilateral restraint in El Obeid would generate a complex attribution and scoring question.

emergingRisks.risk: Somalia's Buur Hakaba district sits at IPC AMN Phase 5 with a GAM rate of 37.1 percent. The end-of-June formal famine declaration window passed without issuance. The famine-declaration risk window now extends through September 2026.
emergingRisks.detail: WFP is reaching approximately one in ten people in Somalia who need food. Al-Shabaab continues to obstruct deliveries. The August lean-season peak will compound the situation. A formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration covering Buur Hakaba or Baidoa, combined with documented government aid obstruction, is the scored change trigger for Somalia's 4.7-of-100 score.

emergingRisks.risk: Venezuela's earthquake response reaches the 14-day checkpoint around July 8. The missing-persons figure stands at 46,600 — described by the New York Times as likely a substantial undercount. Domestic delivery remains slow and access-restricted.
emergingRisks.detail: The two-week mark is the standard window for distinguishing government capacity failure from deliberate aid obstruction or diversion. If documented deliberate obstruction, diversion of relief supplies, or suppression of casualty data occurs before July 8, the Action (ACT) and Accountability (ACC) watch flags reach threshold — downgrade trigger from 18.0 of 100. A demonstrably effective, transparent, and sustained reconstruction effort is the upgrade trigger.

emergingRisks.risk: Yemen's Houthi aid obstruction continues into this cycle without a resolution timeline. WFP's termination of all 365 northern-area staff contracts remains in effect. Doha talks present a possible forward trigger only.
emergingRisks.detail: A durable reversal of the forced WFP staff terminations and release of 73 detained UN workers would constitute scored positive own-conduct for Yemen — the upgrade trigger from 0 of 100. Whether Doha talks produce a humanitarian corridor for Yemen is the observable forward question. Without food-agency presence, delivery to 22.3 million people in need is severely constrained.

emergingRisks.risk: Iran's Strait of Hormuz mining continues with hundreds of vessels stranded and transit fees demanded. If civilian casualties result from Hormuz navigation restrictions, a new threshold event may emerge for Iran's near-floor score of 2.5 of 100.
emergingRisks.detail: The mined central channel and transit-fee demands constitute Iran's own negative conduct already priced in the 2.5-of-100 score. A distinct civilian mass-casualty event from Hormuz navigation — not yet in evidence — would be a new scored threshold event. Doha talks held on June 30 without Tehran confirmation.

emergingRisks.risk: Kuwait's EQU watch flag remains open on bidoon and migrant-worker emergency protection. No emergency-response documentation as of June 30 has addressed Kuwait's approximately 100,000 stateless bidoon residents or its large migrant-worker population.
emergingRisks.detail: Documented discriminatory denial of emergency protection to stateless or migrant populations during wartime would be scored negative own-conduct — a downgrade trigger from 25.0 of 100. A documented equal emergency-protection response extending explicitly to bidoon and migrant workers would be positive own-conduct. The watch flag runs through active Iran-war conditions.

emergingRisks.risk: The Sahel calibration flag is in its seventh consecutive day. Mali holds a starting-point seed score of 12.5 of 100 compared with Burkina Faso's reweighted 6.3 of 100 on comparable UN-documented state-perpetration conduct. No coordinator review timeline is set.
emergingRisks.detail: The coordinator-level review covers Mali, Niger, and potentially Chad. Mali's enforced-disappearance and extrajudicial-killing pattern — documented by UN Special Procedures as potentially constituting crimes against humanity — remains unresolved in the calibration framework. The gap has persisted through the entire June 2026 series without narrowing.

emergingRisks.risk: The EU AI Act becomes fully applicable on August 2 — 33 days from June 30. General-purpose AI transparency obligations, Article 50 labeling, and whistleblower protections activate. xAI and OpenAI have not published compliance roadmaps.
emergingRisks.detail: A post-applicability non-compliance finding for general-purpose AI models constitutes a Tier-5 methodological finding and would move scores. Anthropic's compliance infrastructure is documented. OpenAI's US-government-mandated restricted GPT-5.6 release (June 29) is US government conduct, not OpenAI own-conduct; logged for next rotation. The compliance gap between labs will become legally significant on August 2.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — DRC Ebola (Fourth Province, Cross-Border Risk Activated)
sectorAlerts.headline: Ebola's spread to Haut-Uele on June 30 is the most consequential new development in the June series — it opens the first epidemiological frontier between two floor-level countries.
observations: Case progression from 1,155/304 on June 25 to 1,307/377 on June 30 — described as the fastest-growing Bundibugyo outbreak on record. The outbreak now spans four provinces, with approximately 15 million people in the affected zone.

Haut-Uele borders South Sudan (0 of 100) and the Central African Republic. This is the first scenario in the June 2026 series where two floor-level countries share an active epidemiological frontier. Uganda has recorded 19 cases.

DRC holds at 2.3 of 100. The floor-crossing trigger — documented deliberate government obstruction of WHO response corridors — has not occurred. Worsening transmission dynamics (45 percent contact tracing, no approved vaccine, community burial resistance) are largely exogenous to deliberate government conduct.
The System Capacity (SYS) and Action (ACT) dimensions are the active watch dimensions. M23's eastern harm continues to be attributed to Rwanda, not DRC.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Israel/Lebanon Ceasefire Arc (Day 3 Post-Framework Collapse)
sectorAlerts.headline: Israel launched 100-plus airstrikes on Lebanon on ceasefire-renewal day — 83 killed, 141 wounded; attribution holds; Lebanon scored on own conduct only.
observations: The June 28 Washington framework is definitively closed as of June 30. Hezbollah's rejection on June 30 is the final confirmation. Israel resumed and escalated strikes, killing 83 and wounding 141 in a single day.
Attribution holds: Israel's strikes are Israel's own negative conduct. Lebanon is assessed on its own government conduct only. No new Lebanese own-conduct either direction occurred June 30.

Israel reinforced at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. Lebanon confirmed at 17.2 of 100. The Boundaries (BND) and Action (ACT) dimensions are the active watch dimensions for Lebanon: Lebanese Armed Forces sovereign control versus Hezbollah, and civilian protection under continued strikes.

Forward trigger for Lebanon upgrade: a new ceasefire agreement with Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control and reconstruction access. That process must begin again from zero after the June 28 framework collapse.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Floor Cluster (Full Eight-Entity Documentation, First Time in Series)
sectorAlerts.headline: All eight Critical-band floor and near-floor entities documented in one cycle June 30 — scores stable, threshold events unresolved, methodology holding.
observations: Sudan (0 of 100): El Obeid siege Day 18-plus; al-Burhan ceasefire rejection June 29 stands; US warns of imminent mass-atrocities risk. No new June 30 development.
Israel (0 of 100): 83 killed from 100-plus Lebanon strikes on ceasefire-renewal day June 30 — floor reinforced.
Yemen (0 of 100): WFP 365 staff terminated; 73 UN workers detained; Doha talks are a forward factor only. No new June 30 development.
South Sudan (0 of 100): DRC Ebola spread to Haut-Uele creates new cross-border forward risk. Exogenous at this stage — not yet scored own-conduct.
DRC (2.3 of 100): Fastest-growing Bundibugyo outbreak on record; 4th province June 30; 1,307/377 cases/deaths.
Iran (2.5 of 100): Hormuz mined; transit fees demanded; 784-plus executions year-to-date (37-year high); Doha talks without Tehran confirmation.
Somalia (4.7 of 100): First priority assessment; Buur Hakaba IPC AMN Phase 5; GAM 37.1 percent; end-of-June famine window closed without declaration.
Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100): Sahel lean-season crisis; Sahel calibration flag day seven. All eight entities hold at published scores. No threshold events cleared this cycle.

sectorAlerts.headline: Toll rises to 1,719 dead and 46,600 missing on Day 6; UNICEF 48-tonne shipment arrived; US commitment doubled to 300 million dollars; score holds at 18.0 of 100.
observations: Venezuela (18.0 of 100): Death toll rose from approximately 1,450 on June 29 to 1,719 on June 30. Reported missing reached 46,600. The New York Times characterized the figure as a likely substantial undercount — an early allegation, not documented suppression.

UNICEF's second supply shipment (48 tonnes) arrived June 30; first 20-tonne shipment arrived June 27. US doubled its commitment to 300 million dollars. Both are donor actions, not Venezuelan government conduct.

Aid acceptance from international sources continues — this separates Venezuela from floor-cluster states like Yemen. Domestic delivery remains slow, opaque, and access-restricted. The scoring balance: both upgrade and downgrade triggers remain unmet.
July 8 is the next decisive checkpoint — the 14-day mark from the June 24 earthquake. The Action (ACT) and Accountability (ACC) watch flags remain open.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Gulf States Under Active Iran War (Day 3)
sectorAlerts.headline: Kuwait, Bahrain, and Qatar confirmed at live baselines; Doha stand-down holding Day 3; no new attacks; Gulf attribution chain operating consistently.
observations: Kuwait (25.0 of 100): No new attack June 30. Doha stand-down holding. Desalination plants remain exposed to the Hormuz conflict — a threat from Iran's conduct, not harm Kuwait inflicted. EQU watch flag (bidoon and migrant emergency protection) remains open.

Bahrain (9.4 of 100): No new attack June 30. Stand-down holding. No documented wartime crackdown or discriminatory denial of protection June 30. EQU and ACC watch flags (Shia-majority wartime emergency powers, political prisoners) remain open.

Qatar (10.9 of 100): Qatar hosted US-Iran Doha technical talks June 30 through Qatari mediators (Gharibabadi and Witkoff). Qatar's mediation is mild positive own-conduct consistent with its established diplomatic posture — sub-threshold for a score change. Tehran did not confirm the talks.

The attribution principle continues operating under active war conditions: Iran's June 28 attacks are Iran's own negative conduct. Target governments are scored on their own response conduct only. All three confirmed at live baselines with delta 0.0.

methodologyNotes.name: Full Floor and Near-Floor Cluster in One Cycle — First Time in June Series
methodologyNotes.description: June 30 is the first cycle in the June 2026 series where all eight Critical-band entities at or near the absolute floor were assessed simultaneously. Four hold at 0 of 100 (Sudan, Israel, Yemen, South Sudan) — the floor limitation applies; no lower score is possible. Four hold near the floor (DRC at 2.3, Iran at 2.5, Somalia at 4.7, Burkina Faso at 6.3) — the near-floor limitation applies; a downgrade requires documented deliberate new harm clearing approximately 5 points. The DRC 4th-province Ebola expansion did not cross the Action (ACT) threshold because worsening transmission dynamics (community burial resistance, contact-tracing capacity failure) are largely exogenous to deliberate government conduct. The Haut-Uele / South Sudan epidemiological frontier is logged as the highest-priority new forward risk. It is a precondition for a scored event, not the scored event itself.

methodologyNotes.name: Attribution Principle — Dual-Front Application, First Time in Series
methodologyNotes.description: June 30 is the first cycle in the June 2026 series where two distinct attribution chains operated simultaneously. Front 1 (Iran-Gulf, Day 3): Iran's June 28 attacks on Kuwait, Bahrain, and Qatar are Iran's own conduct. Gulf targets are scored on their own response conduct only. All confirmed at live baselines. Front 2 (Israel-Lebanon): Israel's 100-plus airstrikes on Lebanon June 30 are Israel's own conduct. Lebanon is scored on its own conduct only. Lebanon confirmed at 17.2 of 100. Both chains applied the same directionality rule (§3e-bis(3)): entities surfaced on a negative-theater event where the cause is attributed elsewhere are not upgraded for competent response and not downgraded absent new own-harm. No conflict between the two attribution chains. Both resolved to delta 0.0. This is the highest-complexity attribution run in the June series.

methodologyNotes.name: Recovery Run — Pipeline Resilience Note
methodologyNotes.description: The June 30 cycle was completed as a recovery run after the pipeline crashed mid-assessor, having completed 10 of 15 priority assessments. The five missing entities (Burkina Faso, South Sudan, Sudan, Somalia, Yemen) were completed in the recovery pass without re-assessing or overwriting the prior 10 completed assessments. All 15 live-index baselines were verified before the recovery run commenced. Math hygiene validator: 0 errors, 0 drift flags above 0.5 points. All 15 entities assessed at confirmed live index values. All deltas 0.0. The recovery run resolved fully within the cycle window.

forwardTriggers.trigger: A realized Ebola incursion from DRC's Haut-Uele province into South Sudan, combined with documented failure of South Sudan's own emergency response, would constitute scorable own-conduct — the first cross-floor epidemiological trigger in the series.

An implemented, civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed aid access is scored positive own-conduct. RSF commencement of a direct ground assault on El Obeid residential zones — following the El-Fasher pattern — is the next floor-reinforcement event. Al-Burhan's June 29 rejection closes the near-term SAF-side ceasefire path.

A new ceasefire agreement AND Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control of southern Lebanese territory AND reconstruction delivery together constitute scored positive own-conduct for Lebanon at 17.2 of 100. The Rubio framework arc is closed. A new diplomatic process is required.

14-day reassessment checkpoint — standard interval for distinguishing capacity failure from deliberate obstruction. Documented aid obstruction, diversion, or suppression of casualty data is the downgrade trigger from 18.0 of 100. Sustained effective transparent relief is the upgrade trigger. Action (ACT) and Accountability (ACC) watch flags open.

Documented discriminatory denial of emergency protection to stateless bidoon or migrant workers during wartime — Equity (EQU) downgrade trigger from 25.0 of 100. Durable and independently verified expansion of bidoon rights or citizenship recognition — EQU upgrade trigger.

Documented wartime crackdown, mass detention of Shia-majority population, or discriminatory denial of emergency protection — EQU and Accountability (ACC) downgrade trigger from 9.4 of 100. Genuine verified release of political prisoners or restoration of civil and political rights — upgrade trigger.

Durable reversal of Houthi-forced WFP staff terminations and release of all 73 detained UN workers would be scored positive own-conduct — the upgrade trigger from 0 of 100. Whether Doha talks produce a humanitarian corridor for Yemen is the observable forward question.
Full resolution-phase reassessment due. Conversion trigger: reversal of the June 8 codified-impunity law and release of detained union leaders.

July natural Bundibugyo Ebola peak window — active. Outbreak spread to fourth province Haut-Uele on June 30, now bordering South Sudan. Documented government obstruction of WHO response corridors is the floor-crossing trigger toward 0 of 100.
EU AI Act full applicability — general-purpose AI transparency obligations, Article 50 labeling, and whistleblower protections activate. No public compliance roadmap from xAI as of this cycle.

EU AI Act full applicability — same obligations apply. No public compliance roadmap from OpenAI as of this cycle. US-government-mandated restricted GPT-5.6 release (June 29) is US government conduct, not OpenAI own-conduct; logged for next rotation.

EU AI Act full applicability — 33 days away. Anthropic's compliance infrastructure is documented. Fable 5 remains suspended; Mythos 5 partially unblocked for US organizations. Day 19 of the export-control order.
August IPC lean-season update — IPC Phase 5 Borno trajectory and government response adequacy. Actual WFP food-assistance cuts and a realized Phase 5 famine declaration would be scored triggers.

Famine declaration window open through September 2026. Buur Hakaba and Baidoa both at IPC AMN Phase 5. End-of-June formal declaration threshold passed without issuance. Each new formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration in Somalia is a scored change trigger for the 4.7-of-100 score.

Coordinator-level Sahel-band calibration review — Mali (12.5 of 100) versus reweighted peer Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100) on comparable UN-documented state-perpetration conduct. Flag in its seventh consecutive day; no timeline set.
42-state attorney general proceedings and Florida criminal investigation — no fixed hearing dates; any concluded finding, settlement, or criminal charge constitutes a scored change trigger.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: If the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak crosses from DRC's Haut-Uele province into South Sudan, does a documented failure of South Sudan's emergency response constitute scorable own-conduct on the Action dimension — even when the transmission itself is exogenous?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: DRC at 2.3 and South Sudan at 0 of 100 now share an active epidemiological frontier — yet the methodology distinguishes between exogenous disease transmission and a government's own response failure.
